_info: Moog Minitaur mapping for leap_midi.py — Minitaur default MIDI channel is 1 (index 0).
_hand_notes: Left hand height (palm_y) plays bass notes — low range suits the Minitaur's sub-bass voice.
Right hand height (palm_y) plays lead/melodic notes one octave up.
Left hand grab opens/closes the filter cutoff via CC 74 — squeeze fist to close filter.
Right hand roll sweeps filter resonance via CC 71 — tilt palm for resonance.
Both hands use 'retrigger' so every semitone shift fires a new note-on, fitting monophonic bass.

left_hand._comment: Bass register — C2 to C3. grab axis controls filter cutoff (CC 74).

right_hand._comment: Lead register — C3 to C4. roll axis controls filter resonance (CC 71).

_cc_extra._comment: These CCs cannot be sent automatically by leap_midi.py's current hand states.
_cc_extra._comment2: They are documented here for manual mapping or future script extension.

LFO_rate.note: Map to palm_z or a second CC output

glide_rate.note: Map to sphere_radius for on-the-fly glide

gestures._comment: Gesture notes use the Minitaur's lower register as percussion / accent triggers.

swipe_left._comment: Swipe left — root note C2, hard accent. Good for kick/bass hit.

swipe_right._comment: Swipe right — fifth above root (G2), medium velocity.

circle._comment: Circle gesture — octave C3, soft. Use for sustained sub-bass swell.

key_tap._comment: Downward finger tap — D2, loud. Snappy staccato attack.

screen_tap._comment: Forward finger tap — A2, medium. Good for off-beat accents.

_setup_tips: Set Minitaur to MIDI channel 1 (default) — matches midi_channel: 0 above.
Enable MIDI CC receive on the Minitaur: hold MASTER VOL + CUTOFF on power-up.
For expressive filter sweeps: left hand grab (fist=closed filter, open palm=bright).
For resonance: tilt right palm flat (low resonance) to vertical (high resonance).
Glide: send CC 65 value 127 from an external controller or DAW to enable glide,
then CC 93 sets the glide rate — pair with palm_z in a future cc_extra block.
The Minitaur is monophonic — only one hand should play notes at a time for clean output.
Use left hand for bass/root notes, right hand for melodic movement above.
